title: z-lab DFlash drafter recovered + verified against on-disk packed PARO target
target_model: /models/hipengine/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-PARO-full4096-e5-packed-MTP-BF16 (on-disk packed-PARO deployment target; MTP head unused by DFlash)
drafter_model: z-lab/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-DFlash -> /models/hipengine/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-DFlash (905MB, 8-layer qwen3 BF16; NOT a full 35B)

recovery.check1_num_hidden_layers: 40 (MTP head mtp_num_hidden_layers=1 is separate, not double-counted)

acceptance_against_packed_target.note: REALISTIC deployment acceptance vs our PARO-packed/quantized target. Lower than z-lab's FP-target figures (PARO packing shifts the target argmax vs the FP target the drafter trained on) -- the honest number we judge against.

perf_note: speedup_vs_ar = 0.32-0.37x (c1_loop) is dispatch-bound and NOT the deployment number; per #101 the verify is dispatch-bound. The ~1.16x online-gated product figure (reviewer P4) needs the dispatch floor lowered (M14.dispatch.1 / batched path). The gate FUNCTIONS here (low-confidence cycles drop to AR) with exact-AR preserved -- P4 hardening foundation.
verdict: Drafter recovered (0.95GB) and structurally + E2E validated against our on-disk packed target: exact_match_ar=True across plain/multi-prompt/gated, gpu_accept_match_cpu=True. DFlash B=4 avg_accept ~1.89 edges out the MTP head (#100 B=3 ~1.58). The dense online-gated DFlash lane works correctly and is the near-term deployable product lane to harden (P4).
